title: Token Classifier
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program that classifies tokens using pattern matching. Write a function `classify(token)` that takes an object with a `type` field and uses a `match` expression to return a classification string:
- type "number" with value > 0 => "positive number"
- type "number" with value == 0 => "zero"
- type "number" with value < 0 => "negative number"
- type "string" with value.length > 0 => "non-empty string"
- type "string" => "empty string"
- type "bool" => "boolean"
- anything else => "unknown"

Test with these tokens and print each classification on its own line:
{type: "number", value: 42}, {type: "number", value: 0}, {type: "number", value: -7}, {type: "string", value: "hello"}, {type: "string", value: ""}, {type: "bool", value: true}, {type: "other", value: null}
expected_output:
positive number
zero
negative number
non-empty string
empty string
boolean
unknown
notes: Tests pattern matching with guards on object properties. The order of match arms matters - guards must be checked before fallthrough.
